# Conclusion {#sec-conclusion}

This paper has derived a closed-form likelihood for the Poisson stochastic
frontier model with Gamma inefficiency. Integrating the conditional Poisson
against the Gamma yields a PMF expressible as an absolutely convergent
alternating series whose $\alpha = 1$ case reduces to the lower incomplete
gamma function (@eq-pmf-exp), and whose general-$\alpha$ form
(@eq-pmf-gamma) is accurate up to truncation of an infinite series.
The result yields exact maximum likelihood as a tractable alternative to
the simulated likelihood of @feHofler2013, with the same closed-form
treatment available in the cost-frontier orientation and the conditional
technical efficiency score recovered as a ratio of two series. The scaling
extension of @sec-scaling lets observed determinants enter the inefficiency
rate $b_i$ without sacrificing the closed form.

The Monte Carlo evidence in @sec-simulations confirms $\sqrt{n}$ convergence
for all parameters and shows that the exponential frontier ranks units
almost as accurately as the correctly specified Gamma model when the true
shape lies near unity. One limitation follows directly: the model treats the sample as a pooled
cross-section, with $u_i$ independent of $\mathbf{x}_i$; extending the
closed-form likelihood to genuine panel structures with a time-invariant
firm effect distinct from time-varying inefficiency is the most immediate
next step.

The empirical application illustrates the closed-form likelihood in use.
On the @hausman1984 patent panel the exponential frontier is precisely
estimated and reproduces the textbook R&D elasticity, while the
homogeneous Gamma fit cannot separately identify $\alpha$ and $b$. The
scaling specification (@sec-scaling) resolves this difficulty by tying
$b_i$ to firm characteristics, illustrating how the closed form admits
inefficiency determinants without simulation. Two
further extensions are natural: a zero-inflated frontier for settings with
structural non-participation, and Bayesian estimation following
@mutzBornmannDaniel2017 in the count-data SFA setting.
